arts.ttl: Promotion rivals Burnley and Leeds take cold comfort in tense draw
arts.sum: For generations to come in Lancashire and Yorkshire thousands will tell people “I was there” to see the match where the least action in the history of football took place. Burnley and Leeds are supposed to be fighting for promotion but they produced a solitary shot on target between them in a desperate goalless draw. Third-placed Burnley collected their seventh clean sheet in a row in the Championship, while Leeds moved a further point clear at the top of the table. Arguably the result suited both parties but arduous viewing on an extremely cold night at Turf Moor. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Leeds United player ratings vs Burnley as Joe Rodon brilliant but attackers frustrated - Isaac Johnson
arts.sum: Joe Rodon had an excellent game, cutting out balls and playing forward whenever possible. In a close affair, United struggled to find any clear cut chances until Daniel Jame's late effort forced a save from James Trafford.

arts.ttl: Burnley's record-breaking rearguard denies leaders Leeds
arts.sum: Burnley took their run without conceding a goal to more than 10 hours as they held Championship leaders Leeds to a goalless draw at Turf Moor.

arts.ttl: Promotion hopefuls Burnley and Leeds play out drab draw
Burnley 0-0 Leeds United: Both sides happy to take the point in lifeless top-of-the-table clash
arts.sum: Promotion rivals Burnley and Leeds United played out a dull and lifeless goalless draw in a top-of-the-table clash that neither side wanted to lose.

arts.ttl: Leeds' next five Championship fixtures compared to Sheffield United, Burnley and Sunderland
arts.sum: After a dull match in Lancashire, Leeds Live has taken a closer look at the next five Championship fixtures for the Whites as well as their promotion rivals. Both Sunderland and Sheffield United have the opportunity to scupper Leeds' promotion bid should they beat them.

arts.ttl: Burnley v Leeds United: Championship – live
arts.sum: Minute-by-minute updates on the action at Turf Moor Any thoughts and comments, you can email Daniel Email! “You drew the long straw then?” asks Jeremy Boyce. “This is as tasty a match up as you suggested. Top scorers v top defenders. Top of the table v third, in a group of four class teams, two of which will have to go through the nightmare of the playoffs, and they all have experience of that. Is the top of the Championship better than the bottom of the Prem? We’ll find out next season. Burnley need a four-goal win to go top tonight, but I’m thinking a regulation Scott Parker 1-0 would do them. Leeds will need all of their 66% possession to break down the Clarets’ defence. Fast start? Solomon four mins? We’ll find out shortly.” I can’t see Burnley shutting Leeds out a second time – they won 1-0 at Elland Road earlier in the season – nor can I see anyone finishing above Leeds. They just have too many ways of scoring. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Leeds United team news vs Burnley as Daniel Farke makes key Ilia Gruev decision
arts.sum: Daniel Farke has replaced Joe Rothwell with Ilia Gruev in his Leeds United starting XI to take on Burnley in tonight's Championship clash. Sam Byram, who has been a reliable fill-in at left-back, starts in a back line of Jayden Bogle, Ethan Ampadu and Joe Rodon, who has begun every league match this season.

arts.ttl: Burnley team news vs Leeds United as Scott Parker makes one key change for crunch promotion clash
arts.sum: Scott Parker makes one key change to his Burnley side for tonight’s big promotion showdown with Leeds United

arts.ttl: Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain transfer stance emerges as Leeds United 'plot stunning deal'
arts.sum: Leeds United have been linked with a stunning move for ex-Arsenal and Liverpool midfielder Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain before the January transfer window slams shut. However, the opportunities have dried up for the 31-year-old and Oxlade-Chamberlain is believed to be keen on a transfer to return to the Premier League .
